name: transportation_predictive_queue_begins_kestrel_rig_utl
premise: A Transportation Technology response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving transportation predictive queue begins kestrel rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Marco Berg
agents.identity: I am Marco Berg, accountable for a defensible call in Transportation Technology when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Inez Santos
agents.identity: I am Inez Santos, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Transportation Technology.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Marco Berg must decide under rights vs utility. What does Marco Berg do?
